title: Severe emotional flooding with co-handling after failed repair
scenario_summary: Two divorced parents keep slipping into emotionally flooded retellings of earlier relationship injury whenever they try to discuss a present parenting issue. Both still want mediated resolution, but bounded repair no longer stabilizes the conversation, and the right next step is human co-handling rather than pretending the discussion is still autonomously workable.
intended_challenge_type: Escalation calibration between emotional intensity that remains workable and emotional flooding that requires co-handling after failed repair.

public_goals: Reach a workable parenting discussion without reliving the whole marriage every time.
Be taken seriously about the emotional impact of the conflict.
Keep the current parenting issue from collapsing into another unproductive spiral.
interest_profile: Not being retraumatized by the mediation format
starting_positions: Primary position: the process keeps reopening emotional injury and cannot keep going this way.
Secondary position: a human mediator may be needed if the conversation cannot stabilize.

public_goals: Discuss the current parenting issue without being pulled entirely into relationship-history replay.
Keep the process emotionally steady enough to continue.
Still reach mediated resolution if a workable human-supported path exists.
starting_positions: Primary position: the conversation is no longer stabilizing through the same repair attempts.
Secondary position: human mediation support may be necessary if the parenting issue is going to be addressed productively.
